Come celebrate Pride Month at our Rainbow Day – a fun-filled program that celebrates the 2SLGBTQ+ community with crafts, games and music! For families with kids 12 and under. In this program, we’ll decorate our own t-shirts, tote bags, and/or bandanas with a variety of fabric paints and markers. All materials will be provided while supplies last. Participants are also welcome to bring their own items from home to decorate. Drop-in, no registration

events.title: Rainbow Storytime and Craft

The Storytime will include a range of inclusive books, stories, songs and rhymes that foster acceptance, embrace diversity and help kids get ready for reading! Stay after Storytime to make a fun craft (while supplies last).   Drop in, no registration is required. Participants will be capped at the first 30 families .

events.title: Button Making with Special Collections
events.description: Celebrate Pin-Back Button Day with Special Collections!  Make your own button with a button making machine using images from our Digital Archive ( digitalarchive.tpl.ca ).  Add your own design or drawings.  Two button sizes available! Buttons made using images of items from the Baldwin Collection of Canadiana. Left: Photograph of Toronto Public Library, Mechanics' Institute building (1890). Right: Map plate from Ortelius' atlas Theatrvm orbis terrarvm (published 1570).

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Introduction to Design Thinking Building Challenge
events.description: Have you ever wondered what it would be like to be an inventor?  Have you ever thought about building something yourself?  What is something you would like to build?  Is there a problem you would like to fix?  Use your imagination and brainstorm creative solutions to develop a machine that will fix a problem or make a task easier for you!  Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 4 to 8 with their parents/caregivers.

These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Engineering with Responsibility. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Sew Cool Kids:  Cross-Stitch
events.description: This fun and creative 4 week program introduces children aged 6 - 12 to the art of cross-stitching! Kids will learn the basic stitches, how to follow simple patterns, and how to use colorful threads to bring a design to life.  By the end of the program, each child will have completed a bright and cheerful stitched picture to proudly take home and display. Registering will register your child for all 4 sessions.

events.title: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life Program
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the palaeontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! Registration is required.  Please register online or call the branch at 416-393-7700. events.title: Saturday Movie: The Secret Garden
events.description: Presenting the film version of  The Secret Garden , published in 1911 by Frances Hodgson Burnett, a British/American beloved author of children's literature. This classic novel centers on the physical and emotional transformation of two sickly, neglected children through the healing power of nature and friendship. This is the 2020 Disney version of the film starring Colin Firth.  Everyone is welcome to this inclusive screening. Drop-in! No registration required.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

Ingredient consideration: Gluten and dairy This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: 3D Fundamentals with Blender Part III: 3D Animation
events.description: In this part of the Blender 3D Fundamentals Series, participants will learn how to use and understand 3D animation tools such as armatures, keyframes, the dopesheet and graph editor to bring their 3D model to life. Registration is required. Computers will provided for this class. events.title: Employment Summer Workshop
events.description: The Canadian Training Institute is thrilled to present a two- day series that will introduce participants to employment and job search skills for future success Topics will include: Resume Basics Job Search Strategies Interview Skills and Preparation Volunteer hours can be provided to participants upon completion of the camp.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Creating Parts With Machinery This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Vision Systems in Robotics. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.
